pattern: Since the only useful move is turning some other value into the target inside one contiguous window, scoring +1 for that value and -1 for the target and running Kadane's max-subarray over every candidate value turns the single-operation puzzle into 50 tiny maximum-subarray problems.
hints: The operation only ever helps by converting some OTHER value v (not k) into k inside one contiguous subarray. Try every v from 1 to 50 as the value you shift into k.

For a fixed v, walk the array scoring +1 when you see v (a gain, since it becomes k) and -1 when you see k (a loss, since it gets shifted away if it falls inside your chosen window). The best window for this v is the maximum subarray sum, allowing an empty window (never go below 0).

Track the best Kadane gain across all 49 candidate values, and add it to the count of k already present in nums (an empty/no-op window is always an option, so the answer is never less than the existing count of k).

edge_inputs.description: single element requires a full shift
all elements already equal the target
values and target at the opposite ends of the allowed range
target is the maximum allowed value and never occurs
the existing frequency of the target already beats any shift
two separate clusters where only one cluster is worth converting

stress.description: random values within [1,50] and a random target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100000)))
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, k]
